steps.title: Identify the linear scale factor
steps.content: The problem states that each side length of triangle ABC is 4 times the corresponding side length of triangle DEF. This means the linear scale factor from DEF to ABC is 4.

steps.title: Apply the area scaling rule for similar figures
steps.content:
When two similar figures have a linear scale factor of $k$, their areas are related by a scale factor of $k^2$.

Since the linear scale factor is 4:
$$\text{Area}(\text{ABC}) = 4^2 \times \text{Area}(\text{DEF})$$
$$270 = 16 \times \text{Area}(\text{DEF})$$

steps.title: Solve for the area of triangle DEF
steps.content:
Divide both sides by 16:
$$\text{Area}(\text{DEF}) = \frac{270}{16}$$
Simplify this fraction by dividing both numerator and denominator by 2:
$$\text{Area}(\text{DEF}) = \frac{135}{8} \text{ square inches}$$
